title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
text: covariates regressed full dataset cross-validation, held-test information leaks training pipeline. can inflate distort --sample performance. cpmr, leakage-safe behavior means: fit covariate regression fold’s training split ; apply learned regression parameters train test data inside fold; keep parameter-learning steps (example scaling model selection) inside CV well.

title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
code: set.seed(123) n <- 80 p <- 120  # Synthetic connectivity matrix (rows = subjects, cols = edges) conmat <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), nrow = n, ncol = p)  # A synthetic covariate (for example age-like nuisance variable) covariates <- matrix(rnorm(n), ncol = 1)  # Build behavior with both signal and covariate effects edge_signal <- rowMeans(conmat[, 1:10, drop = FALSE]) behav <- 0.7 * edge_signal + 0.6 * covariates[, 1] + rnorm(n, sd = 0.5)  # Leakage-safe CV call: covariates are handled fold-wise inside resampling fit <- fit_resamples(   cpm_spec(),   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav,   covariates = covariates,   kfolds = 5 )  summary(fit) #> CPM resample summary: #>   Number of folds: 5 #>   Performance: #>     Combined: 0.277 (SE 0.132) #>     Positive: 0.303 (SE 0.129) #>     Negative: -0.270 (SE NA)

title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
text: Using reusable cpm_spec() object helpful want CPM settings drive single-fit resampling analyses.
code: spec <- cpm_spec()  single_fit <- fit(   spec,   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav,   covariates = covariates )  resamples_fit <- fit_resamples(   spec,   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav,   covariates = covariates,   kfolds = 5,   return_edges = "sum" )  summary(resamples_fit) #> CPM resample summary: #>   Number of folds: 5 #>   Performance: #>     Combined: 0.214 (SE 0.120) #>     Positive: 0.235 (SE 0.120) #>     Negative: -0.115 (SE 0.080) #>   Selected edges: #>     Positive: 1.50% #>     Negative: 0.33% head(resamples_fit$predictions) #>   row fold       real        both         pos           neg #> 1   1    4 -0.5862850 -0.13713172 -0.13713172  1.647987e-17 #> 2   2    4  0.7262082 -0.06258281 -0.06258281  1.647987e-17 #> 3   3    2  0.4776754  0.11181262  0.08055321  6.622293e-02 #> 4   4    3  0.4263198  0.14677926  0.14677926 -1.734723e-17 #> 5   5    5 -1.5329525 -0.07744804 -0.07744804 -1.040834e-17 #> 6   6    3  0.4996146  0.56813333  0.56813333 -1.734723e-17 dim(resamples_fit$edges) #> [1] 120   2

title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
text: large feature spaces, fold-wise edge storage can grow quickly. Use return_edges = "sum" keep fold-aggregated edge counts. Use return_edges = "none" need predictive performance.

title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
text: Use checklist building CPM workflows: Split train/test folds parameter-learning step. Fit covariate regression training split . Apply training-fitted covariate regression fold’s test split. Keep scaling feature selection inside fold. comparing multiple settings, use nested CV model selection. Report CV design covariate strategy explicitly.

title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
text: Snoek L, Miletic S, Scholte HS (2019). control confounds decoding analyses neuroimaging data. NeuroImage. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuroimage.2018.09.074 Scheinost D, Noble S, Horien C, et al. (2019). Ten simple rules predictive modeling individual differences neuroimaging. NeuroImage. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuroimage.2019.02.057 Rosenblatt M, Tejavibulya L, Jiang R, Noble S, Scheinost D (2024). Data leakage inflates prediction performance connectome-based machine learning models. Nature Communications. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-024-46150-w

title: Native Getting Started with cpmr
text: cpmr’s primary workflow native, matrix-first API built around cpm_spec() together fit() fit_resamples(). vignette walks : shape CPM inputs; fit single native CPM model; run leakage-safe resampling; inspect predictions selected edges; custom resamples missing-data handling work.

title: Native Getting Started with cpmr
text: CPM expects one row per subject one column per edge. conmat: n x p connectivity matrix; behav: length-n numeric outcome vector; covariates: optional n x q nuisance matrix.
code: library(cpmr)  withr::local_seed(123) n <- 80 p <- 200  conmat <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), nrow = n, ncol = p) covariates <- matrix(rnorm(n), ncol = 1) behav <- rowMeans(conmat[, 1:12, drop = FALSE]) +   0.5 * covariates[, 1] +   rnorm(n, sd = 0.5)

title: Native Getting Started with cpmr
text: Use fit() cpm_spec() object want native single fit explicit CPM parameters. single fit, fit() stores p x 2 edge-selection mask pos neg columns default. useful inspecting selected network, performance reported summary() still -sample.
code: fit_obj <- fit(   cpm_spec(thresh_method = "alpha", thresh_level = 0.05),   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav,   covariates = covariates )  fit_obj #> CPM results: #>   Call: fit(object = cpm_spec(thresh_method = "alpha", thresh_level = 0.05),  #>     conmat = conmat, behav = behav, covariates = covariates) #>   Number of observations: 80 #>     Complete cases: 80 #>   Number of edges: 200 #>   Parameters: #>     Covariates:       TRUE #>     Threshold method: alpha #>     Threshold level:  0.05 #>     Bias correction:  TRUE summary(fit_obj) #> CPM summary: #>   Performance (Pearson): #>     Combined: 0.706 #>     Positive: 0.620 #>     Negative: 0.458 #>   Selected edges: #>     Positive: 4.00% #>     Negative: 2.50% dim(fit_obj$edges) #> [1] 200   2

title: Native Getting Started with cpmr
text: Use fit_resamples() cpm_spec() object --sample estimate predictive performance. keeps CPM-specific steps covariate handling, edge selection, model training inside resample fold.
code: resample_obj <- fit_resamples(   cpm_spec(),   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav,   covariates = covariates,   kfolds = 5 )  resample_obj #> CPM resample results: #>   Call: fit_resamples(object = cpm_spec(), conmat = conmat, behav = behav,  #>     covariates = covariates, kfolds = 5) #>   Number of folds: 5 #>   Number of observations: 80 #>   Edge storage: none #>   Mean correlations: #>     Combined: -0.075 #>     Positive: 0.029 #>     Negative: -0.181 summary(resample_obj) #> CPM resample summary: #>   Number of folds: 5 #>   Performance: #>     Combined: -0.075 (SE 0.046) #>     Positive: 0.029 (SE 0.054) #>     Negative: -0.181 (SE 0.099)

title: Native Getting Started with cpmr
text: Native resampling results always keep raw observation-level predictions object, summary() derives fold-level performance need aggregate view. default, fit_resamples() uses return_edges = "none" skips edge storage. keeps resampling object light need predictive performance. also want fold-aggregated edge selection rates, request explicitly: predictions returns one row per original observation. na_action = "exclude" removed subjects fitting, rows still present fold value NA. return_edges = "sum", cpmr stores fold-summed edge counts edge. memory matters, keep default return_edges = "none" use return_edges = "" fold-wise edge arrays truly needed.
code: predictions <- resample_obj$predictions  summary(resample_obj) #> CPM resample summary: #>   Number of folds: 5 #>   Performance: #>     Combined: -0.075 (SE 0.046) #>     Positive: 0.029 (SE 0.054) #>     Negative: -0.181 (SE 0.099) head(predictions) #>   row fold       real         both          pos           neg #> 1   1    5 -0.6339456  0.342534590  0.342534590  1.994932e-17 #> 2   2    1  0.4116546 -0.478738601  0.028245399 -5.179443e-01 #> 3   3    1  0.2898583 -0.032769603  0.073529511 -1.070810e-01 #> 4   4    3  0.7870086 -0.001288122 -0.001288122  2.428613e-17 #> 5   5    5  0.1169538 -0.023682986 -0.023682986  1.994932e-17 #> 6   6    3  0.7875511  0.255961736  0.255961736  2.428613e-17 edge_resample_obj <- fit_resamples(   cpm_spec(),   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav,   covariates = covariates,   kfolds = 5,   return_edges = "sum" )  dim(edge_resample_obj$edges) #> [1] 200   2

title: Native Getting Started with cpmr
text: already partition scheme, pass resamples. Custom resamples must: supplied assessment-row indices; cover every complete-case subject exactly ; overlap across folds. na_action = "exclude", indices still refer original row numbers complete-case subjects kept analysis.
code: custom_resamples <- split(   seq_len(n),   cut(seq_len(n), breaks = 4, labels = FALSE) )  custom_obj <- fit_resamples(   cpm_spec(),   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav,   resamples = custom_resamples,   return_edges = "none" )  summary(custom_obj) #> CPM resample summary: #>   Number of folds: 4 #>   Performance: #>     Combined: 0.158 (SE 0.121) #>     Positive: 0.247 (SE 0.129) #>     Negative: 0.026 (SE 0.113)

title: Native Getting Started with cpmr
text: Set na_action = "exclude" want cpmr fit complete cases preserving original row positions outputs. behavior matters need merge predictions back subject-level metadata resampling.
code: behav_with_na <- behav behav_with_na[c(3, 11)] <- NA_real_  na_obj <- fit_resamples(   cpm_spec(),   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav_with_na,   kfolds = 5,   na_action = "exclude" )  na_predictions <- na_obj$predictions na_predictions[na_predictions$row %in% c(3, 11), ] #>    row fold real both pos neg #> 3    3   NA   NA   NA  NA  NA #> 11  11   NA   NA   NA  NA  NA

title: Native Getting Started with cpmr
text: comfortable native path: read workflow-selection article guidance use native cpm_spec() workflow different settings; read leakage-focused article need detailed covariate handling example; keep cpm_spec() object around want explicit reusable parameter object.

title: Choosing a Workflow in cpmr
text: cpmr organized around one native workflow: create cpm_spec() object, use fit() fit_resamples() depending question want answer. main choice different API families. : fit() single -sample CPM fit; fit_resamples() --sample resampling; fit_resamples(..., resamples = ...) already partition scheme want enforce.

title: Choosing a Workflow in cpmr
text: analyses, start cpm_spec() reuse across single-fit resampling calls. workflow best fit care : large edge spaces; repeated resampling; leakage-safe covariate handling; future CPM-specific performance improvements fold-level reuse.
code: library(cpmr)  set.seed(123) conmat <- matrix(rnorm(80 * 200), nrow = 80) behav <- rnorm(80) spec <- cpm_spec()  fit_obj <- fit(spec, conmat = conmat, behav = behav) resample_obj <- fit_resamples(spec, conmat = conmat, behav = behav, kfolds = 5)  summary(fit_obj) #> CPM summary: #>   Performance (Pearson): #>     Combined: 0.508 #>     Positive: 0.300 #>     Negative: 0.430 #>   Selected edges: #>     Positive: 0.50% #>     Negative: 1.00% summary(resample_obj) #> CPM resample summary: #>   Number of folds: 5 #>   Performance: #>     Combined: -0.041 (SE 0.202) #>     Positive: -0.161 (SE 0.160) #>     Negative: 0.025 (SE 0.194)

title: Choosing a Workflow in cpmr
text: Use fit() want inspect one fitted CPM model current data. Use fit_resamples() want --sample performance estimate custom resampling design.

title: Connectome Predictive Modelling in R
text: cpmr package designed connectome predictive modeling (CPM) R. primary workflow native, matrix-first API keeps CPM-specific training, resampling, leakage-safe preprocessing inside package-controlled code. package relies Rfast row-oriented calculation.

title: Connectome Predictive Modelling in R
text: can install released version cpmr CRAN : can install development version cpmr r-universe :

title: Connectome Predictive Modelling in R
text: Shape connectivity matrix subjects--edges matrix, row contains edge vector one subject, pair behavioral vector. native workflow uses cpm_spec() together fit(). Cross-validated resampling uses fit_resamples() specification object:
code: library(cpmr)  withr::local_seed(123) conmat <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 1000), nrow = 100) behav <- rnorm(100) fit_obj <- fit(cpm_spec(), conmat = conmat, behav = behav)  fit_obj #> CPM results: #>   Call: fit(object = cpm_spec(), conmat = conmat, behav = behav) #>   Number of observations: 100 #>     Complete cases: 100 #>   Number of edges: 1000 #>   Parameters: #>     Covariates:       FALSE #>     Threshold method: alpha #>     Threshold level:  0.01 #>     Bias correction:  TRUE summary(fit_obj) #> CPM summary: #>   Performance (Pearson): #>     Combined: 0.676 #>     Positive: 0.595 #>     Negative: 0.387 #>   Selected edges: #>     Positive: 0.70% #>     Negative: 0.20% resample_obj <- fit_resamples(cpm_spec(), conmat = conmat, behav = behav, kfolds = 5)  summary(resample_obj) #> CPM resample summary: #>   Number of folds: 5 #>   Performance: #>     Combined: -0.057 (SE 0.062) #>     Positive: 0.008 (SE 0.089) #>     Negative: -0.036 (SE 0.077) head(resample_obj$predictions) #>   row fold        real        both        pos         neg #> 1   1    1  0.26499342 0.492748241 -0.2058990  0.76974455 #> 2   2    5  1.83074748 0.323127546  0.2918329  0.05747766 #> 3   3    5 -0.05937826 0.901452079  1.1152311 -0.34018567 #> 4   4    3 -0.05320937 0.659647280  0.5558721  0.26308893 #> 5   5    1  0.43790418 0.002067539  0.2977574 -0.37106308 #> 6   6    4  1.33744904 0.659172053  0.5130919  0.37428531 dim(resample_obj$edges) #> NULL

title: Connectome Predictive Modelling in R
text: cpmr now treats native workflow primary package story: use fit(cpm_spec(), ...) fit_resamples(cpm_spec(), ...) native CPM analyses; keep cpm_spec() object around want explicit, reusable parameter object. matters: CPM often needs leakage-safe fold-local preprocessing can benefit future fold-level caching threshold-specific optimization. workloads fit native cpmr runners better generic orchestration layer.

title: Connectome Predictive Modelling in R
text: Please note cpmr project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

title: cpm Fitted Object — cpm
text: cpm object returned fit() fitting cpm_spec() single dataset.

title: cpm Fitted Object — cpm
text: cpm object list following elements: call Matched call used fitting. spec originating cpm_spec object. params Parameter list used fit time. predictions Data frame observation-level outputs columns row, real, , pos, neg. edges Stored single-fit edge mask p x 2 logical matrix pos neg columns. model Trained CPM model components used prediction.

title: cpm_resamples Resampling Object — cpm_resamples
text: cpm_resamples object returned fit_resamples() stores fold-level outputs resampling.

title: cpm_resamples Resampling Object — cpm_resamples
text: cpm_resamples object list following elements: call Matched call used resampling. spec originating cpm_spec object. params Parameter list used resampling run. predictions Data frame observation-level predictions fold IDs. edges Stored edge output based return_edges (NULL/matrix/array). folds List assessment-row indices fold.

what: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
title: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
text: Create lightweight specification object stores modeling parameters required fit connectome-based predictive model later fit().

title: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
code: cpm_spec(   thresh_method = c("alpha", "sparsity"),   thresh_level = 0.01,   bias_correct = TRUE )  # S3 method for class 'cpm_spec' fit(   object,   conmat,   behav,   ...,   covariates = NULL,   na_action = c("fail", "exclude") )  # S3 method for class 'cpm_spec' fit_resamples(   object,   conmat,   behav,   ...,   covariates = NULL,   resamples = NULL,   kfolds = NULL,   return_edges = c("none", "sum", "all"),   na_action = c("fail", "exclude") )

title: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
text: thresh_method, thresh_level threshold method level used edge selection. method set "alpha", edge selection based critical value correlation coefficient. method set "sparsity", edge selection based quantile correlation coefficient, thus network sparsity controlled. bias_correct Logical value indicating connectome data bias-corrected. TRUE, connectome data centered scaled unit variance based training data model fitting prediction. See Rapuano et al. (2020) details. object cpm_spec object. conmat matrix connectome data. Observations row, edges column (assumed duplicated edges removed). behav numeric vector contains behavior data. Length must equal number observations conmat. Note behav also row/column matrix, converted vector using drop(). ... future extension. Currently ignored. covariates matrix covariates. Observations row, variables column. NULL, covariates used. Note vector provided, converted column matrix. na_action character string indicating action missing values found behav. "fail", error thrown. "exclude", missing values excluded analysis kept output. resamples Optional list assessment indices defining resamples. element must integer vector indexing rows conmat. NULL, folds generated kfolds. kfolds Number folds used resamples NULL. NULL, set number complete-case observations (LOOCV). return_edges character string indicating return value selected edges. "none", edges returned/stored. "sum", edge masks summed across folds. "", fold-wise edge arrays stored.

title: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
text: cpm_spec object storing parameters later fitting. fitted cpm object single -sample fit. Single-fit CPM objects always store selected edge mask. cpm_resamples object containing observation-level predictions, resampling folds, optional stored edges.

title: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
code: spec <- cpm_spec(thresh_level = 0.01) spec #> CPM model specification: #>   Threshold method: alpha #>   Threshold level:  0.01 #>   Bias correction:  TRUE  conmat <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 100), nrow = 100) behav <- rnorm(100)

title: Tidy a cpm object — tidy.cpm
text: x cpm object. ... Additional arguments passed summary(). component character vector indicating component tidy.

title: Tidy a cpm object — tidy.cpm
text: tibble columns storing parameters cpm object columns depending component argument: component = "performance": method method used calculate correlation real predicted values. pos correlation real predicted values positive edges. neg correlation real predicted values negative edges. component = "edges": pos logical vector indicating whether edge selected fitted CPM model (positive). neg logical vector indicating whether edge selected fitted CPM model (negative).

title: cpmr (development version)
text: Removed legacy cpm() entry point deprecated confounds alias. Use fit(cpm_spec(...), ...) / fit_resamples(cpm_spec(...), ...) native workflow. fit() now performs single fit; resampling handled fit_resamples(). cpm_resamples longer stores redundant resample metrics, collect_*() helpers removed. Use summary() aggregated resample results predictions, edges, folds raw outputs.

title: cpmr (development version)
text: Added cpm_spec() native interface fit() fit_resamples(). Added summary.cpm_resamples() made single-fit resample result objects consistent. Added native-first documentation, including getting-started vignette reorganized pkgdown reference pages.

title: cpmr (development version)
text: Refactored internals expanded test coverage around fit, summary, edge, resampling behavior. Hardened resample validation fold must retain least 3 complete-case training observations.

title: cpmr 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-11

title: cpmr 0.1.1
text: Renamed internal constant networks includes corr_types inc_edges, respectively, better reflect purpose. addition, documentation mentioned networks updated use correlation types better reflect meaning. Reorganized R/cpm.R cpm() remains entry-point function top, followed print.cpm(), internal helpers grouped . Improved test architecture explicit internal-helper coverage complete-case fold invariants.

title: cpmr 0.1.1
text: Refactored internal cpm() workflow smaller helpers (normalize_inputs, resolve_include_cases, apply_confounds_regression, etc.) preserving user-facing behavior. Removed obsolete internal alias regress_counfounds(). Removed broad Rfast namespace import favor explicit Rfast:: calls. Updated summary.cpm() edge summarization path avoid mutating local object fields.

title: cpmr 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-10-06

title: cpmr 0.1.0
text: Added summary() method summarize results CPM analysis (#8). Added tidy() method tidy results CPM analysis (#10). Support na_action argument cpm() function handle missing values input data (#2).

title: cpmr 0.1.0
text: Added params cpm() output store input arguments (#14). Let "sum" default value return_edges argument. Let first two dimensions edges output edges networks, respectively. Polish print method cpm class.
